prompts: There are three main types of joint structures: fibrous, cartilaginous, and synovial. Fibrous joints are immovable, found in skull sutures. Cartilaginous joints allow limited movement, found in intervertebral discs. Synovial joints are freely movable, most common in the body, including hinge joints (elbow, knee), ball-and-socket joints (shoulder, hip), pivot joints (neck), saddle joints (thumb), planar joints (wrist), and condyloid joints (wrist). Each joint type's structure determines its range of motion and functional role in the body.
